patch.value:
**Rule:** `eslint-plugin-unicorn` / `no-useless-fallback-in-spread`. **Count:** 57 (Maintainability, Minor).

**CodeFactor list:** https://www.codefactor.io/repository/github/unbraind/pm-cli/issues/main?category=Maintainability&groupId=10573

## What it is

Spreading `null`/`undefined` into an **object literal** is a no-op, so a `|| {}` / `?? {}` fallback is dead code:

```ts
{ ...(context ?? {}) }   // ->  { ...context }
```

## Confirmed instances (grep; verify each is an OBJECT spread)

- `src/cli/main.ts:344`, `src/cli/main.ts:385` — `...(context ?? {})`
- `src/cli/help-json-payload.ts:334` — `...(runtimeContext ?? {})`
- `src/cli/help-content.ts:892`
- `src/cli/commands/test.ts:1227` — `...(linkedTest.env_set ?? {})`
- ~57 total across `src/**` — see the CodeFactor list for the complete set.

## Important caveat

The rule applies to **object** spread only. **Array** spread `[...(x ?? [])]` is NOT useless — spreading `undefined` into an array throws — so do NOT strip `?? []` fallbacks. Only remove `?? {}` / `|| {}` inside object literals.

## Fix

Largely auto-fixable (`eslint --fix` once the parity lint exists — see sibling tooling item). Apply, then confirm tests + 100% coverage + typecheck stay green.
CodeFactor reports 0 no-useless-fallback-in-spread findings; full coverage suite (node scripts/run-tests.mjs coverage) stays green at 100%; pnpm typecheck and quality gates pass.
